{"idea": {"Name": "spectral_precision_routing", "Title": "Spectral Geometry-Guided Mixed-Precision Routing for Zero-Training Inference Acceleration", "Short Hypothesis": "The local spectral geometry of layer-wise activation covariances (specifically SVD decay rate and condition number) directly predicts the numerical stability threshold under quantization. By training-free mapping of these geometric invariants to dynamic bit-width allocation, we can enforce strict latency/memory budgets while preserving decision boundary stability, bounded by PAC-Bayes risk guarantees. This direction is necessary because existing quantization methods treat precision sensitivity as static or input-agnostic, ignoring how activation geometry dictates numerical stability. Investigating this via training-free spectral diagnostics isolates the geometric determinants of quantization fragility without confounding training dynamics.
